heroHeadline: Pakistan Secures Indefinite Iran Ceasefire Extension; IRGC Seizes Two Hormuz Ships; Af-Pak Non-Escalation Holds Day 13
heroSubtitle: Trump extended the US-Iran ceasefire indefinitely on April 22 at the request of Pakistan's Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir and PM Shehbaz Sharif, validating Pakistan's role as the world's primary peace broker. Iran's IRGC seized two container ships (MSC Francesca and Epaminondas) in the Strait of Hormuz despite the extension, signaling continued coercive leverage. On the Af-Pak front, the Urumqi non-escalation framework held for a 13th consecutive day — the longest unbroken lull in the conflict — as Pakistan's diplomatic capital surges and Urumqi Round 2 is now expected in mid-to-late May. Afghan inflation reached 7.6% in March 2026 driven by disrupted Iranian imports, and 155,000 Afghans have returned from Iran since the war began, straining western provincial economies.
footerNote: Data sourced from UNAMA, SIGAR, ACLED, Brown University Costs of War Project, US DoD, Reuters, BBC, Tolo News, Dawn, Al Jazeera, HRW, OHCHR
